reviews.review_msg: He's a really nice guy. He's funny, joking every class.  He has his own notes, very well-organized. He gives us feedbacks on time, which i really appreciate. Everytime I went to his office for help, he looked energetic and was helpful. To pass this class is easy. Just make sure you read the textbook and do enough practice.

Keshav is a great calc prof.  This class was tough, but he did a good job.  Tests were fair, was really helpful during office hours and via email.  He is very respectful and very humble. This course requires a lot, lots of homework. Final was scaled.

Math was never my thing but he was a good prof. His examples in class tend to be much easier than the homework questions, but he is available if there is anything you don't understand. I would recommend him.

Midterms have exact same questions/concept as review sheet except with modified numbers. make sure you study those! However, Final exam had very specific things and 20 marks on multiple choice. Very helpful clear at explaining. Overall a good prof.

I'm not a math person but overall it didn't kill me. I really liked Dr. Mukunda because he's funny and friendly and his notes are clear and organized most of the time. His midterms and final are fair and pretty much EXACTLY the same as his review sheets. Would definitely recommend!

Even tho this was the 1st time he taught this course, he knew the material well. He was so confident w/ it that he could explain it 3 different ways so you would understand. Overall fair. Final was hard, he focused on odd concepts & excluded others. Put stuff on there from Math 154. But overall I'd give him an A
Shows reasoning for examples, but seems a little unsure. Do the homework assignments and make sure you do the reviews before midterms.

He has a great personality however that in no way means that he is a good prof when it comes to teaching. He gets off topic very easily and is extremely difficult to follow! His class was extremely frustrating because the notes don't help at all and he doesn't even bother to put up the answers to the reviews before exams! Cannot teach well! :/
Great professor!

Keshav is a super friendly and really funny. His midterms were really fair on the whole. Make sure you study after the first midterm because the material does get harder, though do-able. Overall I'd say Keshav is a good prof, just make sure you keep up. Final is harder than the midterm but mainly just because the material is cumulative.
Great prof! Fun and interesting lectures. Tests were fair and assignments are similar to test material.

Good prof! Exams can be hard if you are not a math guy. Textbook is crucial since the notes and lectures are little bit unclear. Keep up with the reading and DO THE HOMEWORK BY YOURSELF; you will be fine. Final exam emphasizes the topics (especially the application) after the midterm. BTW he looks hot..!
good prof, but his exams are really hard, han the final grade is so unfair!
Too much things to write during the lectures. It's difficult to listen and write at the same time. His lectures are a bit rushed and the tests are difficult.

He is a good prof who would answer all your e-mails/concerns. He is always willing to help in a positive way. Notes are to clarify the book with the baboon and it is printable. Tests are extremely hard and its a race against the clock. First midterm had an average of 48%.
great prof, he was excellent at explaining things until you proved to him you understood. Not sure if he's still teaching math, but if anybody gets the chance to take a class with him, DO IT!
Good Prof.

he explains everything so that you not only know how to do the problems, but so that you understand the concepts as well. and he explains in a such a way that it sticks. for example, he uses visual aids on the computer to demonstrate visually, the concepts which are taught, instead of just proving concepts with abstract equations. great teacher
Took this course at harbour centre.  He has a great sense of humour, explains every single topic in great detail.  Quizzes and exams were fair.  Fairly good professor.  I recommend him.
I'm an international student and I wasted $2000 on this course. Easily the worst investment of my life!
No Comment.
By far the worst mathematics professor at SFU. Unfair and judgemental! Highly unclear and boring!
He has a sense of humor and explains things quite well. I enjoy his classes. Math is tough though so if you don't put in your side of the work you won't do well.
Excellent prof. Very helpful, and understanding. Very very funny. He is one of the best profs at SFU!
Hilarious prof. I've failed math 150 with him and probably will fail 152. Still a hilarious prof. [Dr. Mulholland = best]
Great prof with materials explained pretty well. Go to lectures and ask questions is the way to get good grades.
Nice guy, and very funny :P

He?s a great prof and a great human being in general; he is funny, helpful, and approachable. He explains the material very well (I never really did understand math until KM) and is always available for extra help. The assignments are lengthy at times but that?s for all math classes. The midterms are fair but the final exam is HARD, so be prepared.
exam is hard and little bit unfair. however he is funny prof.

Great professor. Very engaging. Presents the material in a clear and digestible way. Uses lots of examples in lecture, many drawn partially or completely from the text. Great use of supplementary materials in class (interactive graphs and visual reference for word problems). Highly recommend taking a class with him.
Browsing through this site, never revied him so here I go.BEST PROF AT SFU BAR NONE.

Very funny teacher with a lot of random sarcastic comments!  He does a good job of explaining concepts and guides you in the right direction as to what will be on the mid-terms and final.  I hate math but Keshav made it bearable.
Very helpful, easy to interact with, uses real life examples which is usually quite helpful in math.
Very interested in subject matter and tried hard to expalin things in a meaningful way.

A super nice prof. If you've learned calculus before, his class is just piece of cake. But, if havent, his class is helpful and clear. Pretty straight forward. Midterms are easy but fair unless you are lazy.

Very good teacher. Knows what he's teaching and explains concepts really well. Very organized and covers all material very well. Is there to help when you don't understand material. Overall a good guy!

An extremely clear and charistmatic teacher.  Made the material easy to understand, with clear examples.  Class notes are fabulous and a great help.  Also very helpful in the workshop and during office hours.  The best math teacher I have had so far, period.
Good prof with patient, and always glad to help you. Midterms are not easy because of the time limit, but Final is pretty easy.
He's a nice person, but the exams r kinda hard for me :( Overall he is a really good prof

He's pretty funny with a witty sense of humour but as for teaching, who needs lectures when you've got the text? I wish that the material wasn't practically copied and pasted into the class notes. The point of an instructor is to interpret what's in the book in his own way for a better understanding or a better point of view. Totally did not happen
A fairly good teacher. He kept everyone interesting and conveyed the material effectively. Exams seemed easy but fair.
